name: Coach House Black Bean Soup
recipeIngredient: 5 Strips Bacon, cut into small pieces
Rind and Bone from Smoked Ham OR 2 Smoked Ham Hocks, split
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the beans and cover with cold water. Soak overnight.
2. Drain and wash the beans again. Place them in a casserole dish and add 2 1/2 quarts of water. Cover and simmer for 90 minutes.
3. Cook the bacon in a heavy kettle until it is crispy. Add the celery and onions, cooking until they are tender but not browned.
4. Blend in the flour and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
5. Add the ham, beef bones, parsley, bay leaves, garlic, carrots, parsnips, pepper, salt, and beans with their cooking liquid to a large pot. Cover and simmer over low heat, stirring occasionally, for 4 hours.
6. If necessary, add more water to reach the desired consistency.
7. Remove the bone and ham rind or hocks from the soup. Blend half of the soup in a blender and set aside.
8. Remove any meat from the ham bones or hocks, chop it fine, and return it to the soup.
9. Add the blended soup back into the pot.
10. Stir in the wine and chopped eggs until well combined.
11. Garnish with lemon slices before serving.
